events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess trench protection: trench box void behind shield. Work stage tested/inspected. Component condition installed-defective. Need compare against OSHA 1926 Subpart P. Trench box void behind shield means gap between trench box shield and trench wall? In trench box/shield use, OSHA requires shields be installed to protect workers, and voids between shield and excavation face should be filled or protected to prevent cave-ins behind shield? Need recall Subpart P. Trench boxes (trench shields) are protective systems. If there is a void behind shield, soil can cave into void and possibly compromise stability or worker entry? Requirement: Excavations must be protected by sloping/benching/shoring/shielding. Trench shields must be designed to withstand forces. If void exists between shield and excavation wall, material may slough or fall into void; workers between shield and wall? Actually workers are inside shield. Void behind shield means space between shield exterior and trench face. This can allow soil to move/collapse behind shield, potentially causing settlement, undermining, or load concentration. OSHA 1926.652(g)(2): Trench boxes must be placed so that the bottom of the box is no lower than the bottom of the excavation, and the top no lower than top? Also 1926.652(i) says shield system installed to restrict lateral or other movement. Manufacturer tabulated data may require backfilling voids. Competent person must inspect. A void behind shield is a common violation; should be backfilled or use a larger shield. Decision fail. Risk high or critical? Cave-in hazard, potential fatality. In trench, void behind shield could lead to collapse/entrapment. Risk high (or critical). Since dangerous/non-compliant, decision fail. Work_stage tested/inspected. percent_complete maybe 80? Trench protection installed but defective. component_conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Findings: void between trench shield and excavation wall; inadequate soil support; potential cave-in/undermining. Actions: stop work in trench, remove workers, have competent person evaluate, backfill void or install properly sized shield per manufacturer/OSHA, re-inspect before re-entry. References: OSHA 1926 Subpart P, 1926.652(g), 1926.652(i), 1926.651(k)(1) competent person daily inspections. Need JSON only. Ensure valid JSON.

  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "tested/inspected",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 80,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Void exists between the trench box shield and the excavation wall.",
    "The gap eliminates lateral soil support and can allow sloughing, cave-in, or undermining behind the shield.",
    "Workers inside or near the trench are exposed to a potential cave-in hazard."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately stop work in and near the trench and remove all workers from the excavation.",
    "Have the competent person evaluate the void and the shield installation.",
    "Backfill the void or install a properly sized trench box/shield that contacts the excavation walls per manufacturer tabulated data and OSHA requirements.",
    "Re-inspect the protective system and document acceptance before allowing re-entry."
  ],
  "rationale": "OSHA 1926 Subpart P requires protective systems to be installed and maintained so they actually protect workers from cave-ins. A void behind a trench shield means the shield is not bearing against the soil as intended, creating a cave-in/undermining hazard and making the installation defective and non-compliant.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart P",
    "29 CFR 1926.652(g) - Requirements for protective systems",
    "29 CFR 1926.652(i) - Shield systems",
    "29 CFR 1926.651(k)(1) - Daily inspections by a competent person"
  ]
}
